title: x-graphql Attribute Registry
description: Canonical registry of x-graphql extension attributes used by json-schema-x-graphql converters. Each entry describes attribute name, scope, accepted types, default behavior, examples, and recommended usage.

attributes.name: x-graphql-type-name
attributes.description: Explicit GraphQL type name to use when generating the GraphQL type for this JSON Schema object or $ref target. Overrides derived names.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to provide a stable PascalCase type name when the source JSON Schema name is not suitable for GraphQL or to maintain backward compatibility with an existing GraphQL schema.
attributes.notes: Converter should validate that provided name is a valid GraphQL Name and optionally apply namingConvention if configured.

attributes.name: x-graphql-type-kind
attributes.description: Specifies the GraphQL kind to generate for this schema (e.g. OBJECT, INTERFACE). Useful when the schema shape alone is ambiguous.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use when you want to generate non-default GraphQL types (e.g. treat a JSON schema as an interface). Ensure the shape meets GraphQL constraints for the target kind.
attributes.notes: For UNION, use with `x-graphql-union-types` to list members.

attributes.name: x-graphql-implements
attributes.description: List of interface type names that this generated GraphQL type should implement.
attributes.recommendedUsage: When mapping JSON Schema fragments into GraphQL interfaces, declare implementations explicitly rather than relying on inference. Names should correspond to existing or concurrently generated types.

attributes.name: x-graphql-union-types
attributes.description: For a schema that should be emitted as a union, list the member GraphQL type names.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Define unions explicitly; ensure members are available in the same conversion session or previously published schema.

attributes.name: x-graphql-type-directives
attributes.description: Array of directives to attach to the type. Each entry may be a string (directive name) or an object with name and arguments.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for federation-related directives or custom directives required by downstream tooling. Converter must render directive arguments in GraphQL syntax.

attributes.name: x-graphql-description
attributes.description: GraphQL-specific description to use for the type or field. Overrides the JSON Schema 'description' for GraphQL output.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Prefer this when the JSON Schema description contains implementation details not suitable for API docs or when you need a shorter, consumer-facing description.

attributes.name: x-graphql-skip
attributes.description: If true, the type or field should be excluded from GraphQL generation. If a string, it may indicate a reason or a conditional key recognized by converter tooling.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use sparingly. Prefer server-side filtering or dedicated DTOs for shape differences. `x-graphql-skip: true` is appropriate for internal/private fields that must not be exposed. If you need conditional exposure (e.g. based on roles or environment), do not encode dynamic runtime conditions in the schema; instead, use build-time flags or generate separate schema targets.
attributes.notes: Converters should log skipped elements for traceability and provide metrics. Skipping must not produce broken types — if removing a field produces an empty object type and `emitEmptyTypes` is false, the converter may omit the type entirely or convert to `JSON` scalar depending on configuration.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-name
attributes.description: Override name of the generated GraphQL field (camelCase preferred).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use when source property names are not idiomatic in GraphQL or to preserve a specific public API field name. Converter should validate that name is a valid GraphQL Name.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-type
attributes.description: Explicit GraphQL field type override. Can be a scalar name, complex type name, or a structure describing list/non-null wrapper preferences.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to map JSON schema objects to custom scalars (e.g. JSON) or pre-existing GraphQL types. If you require arguments or generics, use converter extension points rather than embedding complex logic here.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-non-null
attributes.description: Force the field to be non-null (`!`) in GraphQL regardless of JSON Schema `required`.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for semantic guarantees. Prefer deriving nullability from `required` where possible to avoid conflicting declarations. If both `required` and `x-graphql-field-non-null` are present, converters should honor `x-graphql-field-non-null`.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-list-item-non-null
attributes.description: When the field is a list, force list items to be non-null (i.e. `[Item!]`).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to indicate list items will never be null. Combine with `x-graphql-field-non-null` to express `[Item!]!`.

attributes.name: x-graphql-nullable
attributes.description: Explicitly mark a field as nullable in GraphQL (overrides `required` or other non-null directives).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use only when you need to override the `required` semantics for GraphQL consumers. Avoid mixing contradictory annotations; if `x-graphql-field-non-null` and `x-graphql-nullable` are both present, converters must document precedence (prefer explicit GraphQL-specific attribute).

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-directives
attributes.description: List of directives to attach to the field. Entries can be strings or objects with name/args.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for schema annotations like deprecation or federation-specific directives. Keep directive usage minimal and documented.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-arguments
attributes.description: Define GraphQL field arguments (name, type, default). Useful for mapping functions or computed fields.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Prefer to reserve arguments for computed or resolver-backed fields. Static data fields usually should not have arguments.

attributes.name: x-graphql-scalar
attributes.description: Map schema value to a custom scalar type in GraphQL (e.g. 'DateTime', 'JSON').
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for well-understood scalar mappings. Ensure scalar is declared/available in the consumer schema or in a shared schema module.

attributes.name: x-graphql-scalars
attributes.description: Map of nested property names to custom scalars for more complex type mappings.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for per-field custom scalar overrides grouped at type-level; easier to manage for large objects.

attributes.name: x-graphql-enum
attributes.description: Controls enum generation: value mapping, name overrides, value descriptions.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to enforce desired GraphQL enum member names, especially when JSON values are not valid GraphQL names.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-arguments-defaults
attributes.description: Convenience object to provide default values for field arguments when defining `x-graphql-field-arguments`.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Include small, deterministic defaults only. Larger defaults or environment-driven defaults are better handled by resolvers.

attributes.name: x-graphql-operations
attributes.description: Hint to the converter about which operation (if any) to expose this type under (e.g. top-level Query fields).
attributes.recommendedUsage: This is a hint only; converters should provide configuration to control how operation hints materialize into top-level fields.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-keys
attributes.description: List of field sets to use as `@key` directives for Apollo Federation entity references.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to mark entity types in subgraphs. Keys must be stable and uniquely identify an entity across the subgraph.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-shareable
attributes.description: Marks a type or field as shareable across subgraphs (`@shareable`).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use in multi-team environments where fields are read by multiple subgraphs. Coordinate with federation design to avoid duplication.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-requires
attributes.description: Indicates fields required by computations (`@requires`) when resolving this field from another subgraph.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to declaratively express dependencies for federation resolvers. Keep requires minimal and explicit.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-provides
attributes.description: Indicates which nested fields are provided to other subgraphs (`@provides`).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use only when a subgraph can produce fields that other subgraphs request; coordinate schema ownership.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-external
attributes.description: Marks a field as external in federation (`@external`).
attributes.recommendedUsage: Used on entity types to reference fields owned by other subgraphs. Keep ownership clear in documentation.

attributes.name: x-graphql-federation-override-from
attributes.description: Indicates a previous source of the field for `@override(from: "other")` semantics.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use sparingly during migration phases where fields move between subgraphs.

attributes.name: x-graphql-preserve-order
attributes.description: Hint to preserve property order from JSON Schema when generating GraphQL fields.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Useful when deterministic ordering is important for diffs or documentation. Converters should respect configuration at global or per-conversion level.

attributes.name: x-graphql-infer-ids
attributes.description: Legacy hint to infer ID scalar for fields named 'id' or '_id'. Prefer `idStrategy` in options.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Deprecated in favor of converter-level `idStrategy`. Use converter configuration to control global heuristics rather than per-field hints.

attributes.name: x-graphql-field-rename-map
attributes.description: Type-level map of original property names to GraphQL names for multiple fields.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use to centralize per-type field renames instead of sprinkling `x-graphql-field-name` on each property.

attributes.name: x-graphql-exclude-types
attributes.description: List of type names or patterns to exclude from generation. Can be used globally or per-type via pattern lists.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Prefer converter-level `excludeTypes` or `excludePatterns` configuration instead of embedding global exclusions in the schema.

attributes.name: x-graphql-exclude-patterns
attributes.description: Regex patterns to exclude fields or types from generation.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for organization-wide rules. Patterns are easier to maintain than per-field annotations when many similar fields exist.

attributes.name: x-graphql-description-block-threshold
attributes.description: Threshold length (characters) at which descriptions should be rendered as GraphQL block-strings.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Set to a conservative value to improve readability of long descriptions in SDL outputs.

attributes.name: x-graphql-emit-empty-types
attributes.description: When false, types with no fields (after skipping/exclusions) are not emitted. When true, emit empty types.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Prefer `false` to avoid empty types in client schemas unless empty types are meaningful in your domain.

attributes.name: x-graphql-inline-object-threshold
attributes.description: Maximum property count for anonymous objects to be inlined as a JSON scalar or converted to an inline GraphQL type.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Lower values produce fewer generated types and more JSON scalars; increase for strongly-typed APIs.

attributes.name: x-graphql-ref-naming
attributes.description: Strategy to name types derived from $ref values.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use `file_and_path` for disambiguation in multi-file projects; use `hash` for deterministic anonymous names when collisions are possible.

attributes.name: x-graphql-metadata
attributes.description: Free-form metadata consumed by tools (e.g. codegen hints). Not interpreted by the converter except when known keys are present.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for tooling-specific annotations. Keep keys namespaced to avoid collisions (e.g. `org.team.href`).

attributes.name: x-graphql-deprecated
attributes.description: Mark field or type as deprecated. If object, may include `reason` string.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for deprecation lifecycle. Converters should emit `@deprecated` with the reason when present.

attributes.name: x-graphql-visibility
attributes.description: High-level visibility hint used by tooling for documentation and client codegen filtering.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Keep visibility orthogonal to `x-graphql-skip`. Use 'internal' for things normally omitted from public clients, and enforce via build-time filters.

attributes.name: x-graphql-arguments-schema
attributes.description: JSON Schema describing the structure of field arguments; helpful for codegen that needs argument validation schemas.
attributes.recommendedUsage: Use for computed fields that accept structured arguments, enabling server-side validation and client SDK generation. Keep small for performance.

skipAttributeGuidance.summary: Prefer explicit exclusion at design-time; avoid using `x-graphql-skip` for conditional runtime behavior.
details: Use `x-graphql-skip: true` to mark fields/types that must never be exposed in GraphQL (e.g. internal-only secrets, implementation metadata).

Avoid encoding runtime conditions (roles, environment, feature flags) in the schema. If you need conditional exposure, generate separate GraphQL artifacts for each target or use build-time flags that generate different GraphQL artifacts.
Document skipped elements: converters should emit a traceable summary of skipped items in conversion logs to aid audits and debugging.

Prefer `x-graphql-visibility` for softer hints (e.g. `internal`) and handle omission via converter configuration (`excludePatterns` or `excludeTypes`) rather than embedding `skip` widely across schemas.
Ensure skipping a field does not produce invalid GraphQL types (e.g. empty types). Use `emitEmptyTypes` and `inlineObjectThreshold` config in tandem to control fallback behavior.
